title: Roll-Out Pantry for Narrow Gaps
summary: A slim rolling pantry sized to slide into the narrow gap beside a fridge or between cabinets — dowel-retained shelves on caster wheels, buildable in any width from 4 in to 12 in.
description:
This pantry is built to fit a specific kind of dead space: the narrow gap that's too small for cabinetry but wide enough to waste if left empty, next to a fridge or between two cabinet runs. **The board width is a genuine choice, not a fixed spec** — the same design works built from 1x4s for a 4" gap up through 1x12s for a 12" gap, and the cut list below is sized for a 6" gap using 1x6 stock; scale every board width up or down to match your own gap and lumber choice.

Cut the sides to your desired height minus the height of your caster wheels, and the shelves to your desired depth minus 2" — these two formulas are the actual plan, more than any fixed number, since every installation's gap dimensions differ. Attach the shelves to the sides with pocket holes first, then cut and attach the plywood back to fit, nailing and gluing to every back edge.

Wood dowels attached to the inside of the sides beneath each shelf keep tall or narrow items (cutting boards, trays, baking sheets) from tipping over as the unit rolls in and out. Caster wheels on the base finish the piece — this is meant to roll, not sit fixed in place, since the whole point is pulling it out into the room to access items along its depth rather than reaching blind into a gap.

materials.name: 1x6, 8 ft (sized for a 6 in gap; substitute 1x4–1x12 to match your gap)
Fixed caster wheels, 2–3 in, with screws
Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping wood screws, 2"
Brad nails or wood trim screws, 1-1/4" and 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1x6, 8 ft (sized for a 6 in gap; substitute 1x4–1x12 to match your gap)
cutList.note: Cut to your desired height minus caster wheel height.

cutList.material: 1x6, 8 ft (sized for a 6 in gap; substitute 1x4–1x12 to match your gap)
cutList.note: Cut to your desired depth minus 2 in. Add or remove shelves as needed.
Cut to match shelf length; one per shelf.

steps.title: Cut the sides and shelves
steps.body: Determine your gap's actual width, height, and depth. Cut the sides to your desired height minus the caster wheel height, and the shelves to your desired depth minus 2 in.
materials: 1x6, 8 ft (sized for a 6 in gap; substitute 1x4–1x12 to match your gap)

steps.title: Attach the shelves to the sides
steps.body: Attach the shelves to the sides using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.
materials: 1x6, 8 ft (sized for a 6 in gap; substitute 1x4–1x12 to match your gap)
Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping wood screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Measure and cut the 1/4" plywood back to fit. Nail and glue to all back edges of the sides and shelves.

steps.title: Add the dowels and casters
steps.body: Attach a dowel to the inside of the sides beneath each shelf. Attach caster wheels to the base of the unit, and add a handle if desired.
materials: Fixed caster wheels, 2–3 in, with screws
Brad nails or wood trim screws, 1-1/4" and 2"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Slim rolling pantry sized to a narrow gap beside a fridge
